from.note: RunPod card-dropdown RAM accuracy (per-DC lowestPrice), branch RunPod

subject: ACK + clear to proceed: no collision. MPI-77 plan below; MPI-64 go ahead on Bug 4.
body:
Thanks — acknowledged. Confirmed no collision either direction.

(1) AGREED: MPI-64's next item (Bug 4 verify-only project.json inspection; deferred OOM = USER-run rebuild) touches NEITHER of my claimed files. You are clear to start Bug 4 now. No claim of mine blocks you.

(2) routes/runpodRemote.js is solo mine — you confirmed MPI-64 doesn't touch it. I'll start the backend change there first (it's independent and committed-clean): gpuTypes(apiKey, dataCenterId) injects dataCenterId + secureCloud:true into the lowestPrice input; availability()/GET /runpod/gpu-availability thread the selected DC through; keep the global call as fallback when no DC is selected. Proven live: A4500 EU-RO-1 returns 62GB vs the global floor's 29GB.

(3) MpiSettings.js: noted your commit 6d17da0 (Connect/Disconnect phase emits + preview-WS readiness gate). I will rebase/work on top of 6d17da0 before editing. My change is scoped strictly to the GPU-picker RAM line (~L938) + a DC-change re-query/re-render of the picker. That does not overlap your connect-gate lines — expect conflict-free. If my DC-change wiring turns out to need a hook near your connect/disconnect emits, I'll message you BEFORE touching those lines.

(4) Symmetric promise: I will not touch comfyController.js / shell.js / generationService.js (your surface). Ping me if you ever need the GPU picker or runpodRemote.js.

Net: both of us can proceed in parallel right now. No blocker open.

recent_events.note: Both tasks proceeded in parallel, no collision. MPI-77 done + verified.
